description: Modern UX Redesign for app.html (all four views: Login/Auth, Passenger Booking, Operator Console, Admin Console). Keep the IraGo brand (navy #0B1F3A, blue #0057B8) but modernize everything: Inter typography, rounded corners, layered shadows, refined spacing, modern interactive states. HARD CONSTRAINTS for every story: (1) never rename/remove element ids; (2) never rename CSS classes referenced by js/app/*.js - grep before touching; (3) never change inline onclick/onkeydown wiring; (4) never edit js/app.bundle.js directly - edit js/app/*.js then run 'npm run build:js'; (5) for CSS-only changes manually bump the ?v= on the css/app.css link in app.html; (6) no smart/curly quotes in JS; (7) do NOT touch marketing pages (index.html etc.) or css/tokens.css - the app redesign lives in css/app.css + app.html only. Full PRD: tasks/prd-modern-ux-redesign.md

userStories.title: Modern design foundation (tokens, typography, base styles)
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to use a modern type scale, rounded corners, and soft elevation so every screen immediately feels current.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Google Fonts Inter (weights 400;500;600;700;800, display=swap) to app.html head with preconnect links; body font-family in css/app.css becomes 'Inter', -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
Update :root tokens in css/app.css: --radius-sm: 8px, --radius-md: 10px, --radius-lg: 14px, --radius-xl: 18px, --radius-2xl: 24px (keep --radius-full)

Replace shadow tokens with layered modern shadows: --shadow-sm: 0 1px 2px rgba(15,23,42,.06); --shadow-md: 0 4px 12px rgba(15,23,42,.08), 0 1px 3px rgba(15,23,42,.05); --shadow-lg: 0 12px 32px rgba(15,23,42,.12), 0 2px 6px rgba(15,23,42,.06); --shadow-xl: 0 24px 60px rgba(15,23,42,.18)
Add new tokens: --focus-ring: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0,87,184,.25); --surface: #FFFFFF; --surface-subtle: #F6F8FB; --border-subtle: #E6EAF0; --transition: .18s cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1)
Page/body background becomes #F6F8FB (also update the hardcoded #F0F4F8 on #login-view)
Add global :focus-visible rule using var(--focus-ring) for button, input, select, textarea, a; do not leave any outline:none without a visible replacement
Spot-check that login card, booking panel, and admin tables did not break (components inherit the token changes)
No element id or JS-referenced class renamed
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (screenshot login and booking views)

userStories.title: Login and auth cards redesign
userStories.description: As a passenger, I want a polished, welcoming sign-in screen so my first impression of IraGo is a modern, trustworthy product.
acceptanceCriteria: #login-view background: subtle calm gradient (e.g. linear/radial from var(--blue-50) to white), not flat gray
.auth-card: var(--radius-2xl) corners, var(--shadow-lg), 32-40px padding, max-width ~420px
All auth inputs: min-height 48px, var(--radius-md), 1px var(--border-subtle) border, white bg, focus = blue border + var(--focus-ring), placeholder var(--gray-400)

.btn-auth-primary: solid var(--blue), white text, 48px height, var(--radius-md), hover = var(--blue-dark) + translateY(-1px) + shadow, active = no lift, disabled = .5 opacity, transition var(--transition)
.btn-google restyled to match: 48px, rounded, subtle border, hover bg var(--gray-50)

.auth-role-switcher becomes a segmented control: pill container with var(--gray-100) bg, active segment white with var(--shadow-sm); keep same button elements, ids, data-role attributes, and switchLoginRole wiring
Auth card h2: 24-28px weight 700; .auth-subtext: 15px var(--gray-500)
Same treatment applied to ALL auth cards: #login-card, #register-passenger-card, #otp-card, #forgot-card, #reset-card
.auth-error and .auth-success styled as soft rounded alert chips (light red/green backgrounds)
Login, registration, and forgot/reset flows still work (no id removed, no handler changed)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (login, register, forgot cards)

userStories.title: Booking view top navigation and service tabs redesign
userStories.description: As a passenger, I want a clean modern app header with clear service switching so navigation feels effortless.
acceptanceCriteria: .booking-nav: white background, subtle bottom border var(--border-subtle), 64px height, comfortable horizontal padding

.service-tabs becomes a segmented pill control: var(--gray-100) container with var(--radius-full); active tab = white bg + var(--shadow-sm) + var(--blue) text/icon; inactive = var(--gray-500) with hover; smooth transition
All four data-service buttons keep their ids, icons, markup structure, and switchService() handlers
.customer-role-badge, .op-role-badge, .admin-role-badge restyled as small rounded chips (soft blue/navy bg, 12px semibold)
.nav-bell and .nav-text-btn get 36-40px rounded hover targets with var(--gray-100) hover bg
.nav-avatar: 36px circle, navy-to-blue gradient bg, white initial, subtle ring on hover
At <=768px service tab labels hide leaving icons (CSS media query); add aria-label attributes to the four service tab buttons in app.html
Operator and admin navs (same .booking-nav class) inherit the treatment and still look right
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (desktop and 375px width)

userStories.title: Booking panel redesign (locations, routes, ride results)
userStories.description: As a passenger, I want the booking panel to feel like a premium mobility app so finding and booking a ride is pleasant and clear.
acceptanceCriteria: .booking-panel: white surface, clean elevation (var(--shadow-lg) or refined border), 20-24px section padding

Location inputs redesigned as one connected rounded container (var(--radius-lg), var(--border-subtle)): pickup and destination inputs borderless inside with an inner divider; keep .loc-dot elements as the left rail dots and add a CSS vertical connector line between them
Focus on either .loc-input highlights the whole container via :focus-within (blue border + focus ring)
.swap-btn becomes a floating circular button (white, var(--shadow-md), 1px border) positioned on the divider; swapLocations() wiring unchanged
.loc-suggest dropdown: var(--radius-lg), var(--shadow-xl), 8px padding, rounded rows with var(--gray-50) hover
Popular routes chips: grep js/app/06-booking.js for the classes rendered into #popular-routes-area and style them as horizontal rounded chips with hover state
.search-btn: full-width, 50px, var(--radius-md), brand blue, hover lift; existing loading state still styled

Ride cards: grep js/app/06-booking.js for classes rendered into #rides-list; style as white rounded cards (var(--radius-lg), var(--border-subtle)) with hover = blue border + var(--shadow-md), selected = 2px var(--blue) border + var(--blue-50) tint, price 18px bold navy, meta 13px var(--gray-500)
.profile-account-card and #drone-panel cards/pills follow the same card language
Booking flow works end to end: search, select a ride, book
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (booking panel with results)

userStories.title: Live tracking panel and map overlay redesign
userStories.description: As a passenger, I want ride tracking to look like a live, premium experience so I trust what is happening with my flight.
acceptanceCriteria: .tracking-panel: var(--radius-xl), var(--shadow-xl), white surface, 20-24px padding
.tracking-progress-bar: slim rounded gradient bar (blue to sky) with smooth width transition

.tracking-steps: 28px circle dots; done = solid blue with white check; active = blue ring + soft pulse animation; pending = gray-200; connector line between steps; labels 11-12px; JS-toggled state classes (done/active) unchanged
.tracking-pilot-card: rounded var(--gray-50) card, 44px gradient avatar circle, clear name/meta hierarchy
.tracking-otp-card: prominent var(--blue-50) bg or dashed blue border; OTP digits 28px bold monospace with letter-spacing
.tracking-eta: ETA number 24px+ bold; .tracking-status-dot gets a soft pulse animation
.tracking-rate-stars button: 28px+, gold hover/selected color, scale transition
.route-info-bar and .booking-map-legend: floating rounded chips over the map (white bg, var(--shadow-md))
.btn-end-tracking and #tracking-cancel-btn: secondary/outline and danger button variants matching the new button language
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Payment, confirmation and overlay modals redesign
userStories.description: As a passenger, I want checkout and confirmations to feel crisp and trustworthy so paying is friction-free.
acceptanceCriteria: .confirm-overlay backdrop: rgba(15,23,42,.5) with backdrop-filter blur(4px) and a solid-color fallback; subtle fade-in
.payment-page and .confirm-card: var(--radius-2xl), var(--shadow-xl), entrance animation (fade + slight translate/scale, ~200ms) wrapped so prefers-reduced-motion disables it
Payment step indicator modernized (keep #payment-dot-1 and #payment-dot-2 ids)
.payment-fare-breakdown: clean list rows, label left var(--gray-500), value right, var(--border-subtle) dividers, emphasized total row
Coupon chips (grep js for #coupon-chips generated classes): rounded outline chips with applied/active state in blue; coupon input row modernized
.payment-summary-card: var(--blue-50) rounded card with large amount
.confirm-check: 64px green circle with white check and pop-in animation (reduced-motion safe)
.confirm-details cells: soft rounded var(--gray-50) tiles
#must-reset-overlay card inherits the same modal language
Toasts: grep js/app/*.js for toast markup classes; style rounded var(--radius-lg), var(--shadow-lg), colored left accent per type, slide-in animation
Profile modal (opened via .js-open-profile - find its markup in app.html): inherits the same modal styling
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (payment and confirmation overlays)

userStories.title: Operator (pilot) console redesign
userStories.description: As a pilot, I want a clean modern console so my duty status and trip queue are instantly readable.
acceptanceCriteria: .op-welcome: 22-24px bold navy page header; .op-welcome-sub: var(--gray-500)
.op-duty-card: white rounded card (var(--radius-lg), var(--shadow-md)); duty status text green when on duty, gray when off
.op-duty-switch: modern iOS-style toggle, 48x28px track, sliding knob with var(--transition), green track when on; keep id, aria-pressed, and toggleOperatorDuty() wiring

Operator trip/dispatch cards: grep js/app/04-operator.js for generated classes; style as rounded cards with colored status chips per trip state, clear route display, primary accept + outline/danger reject buttons
.op-section-title: eyebrow label style (12px uppercase, letter-spacing, var(--gray-400))
.op-back: subtle rounded back link with hover
Empty states (.op-empty-sub and similar): centered, muted styling
Operator profile card matches the new card language
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Admin console navigation drawer and dashboard redesign
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a modern dashboard shell so the console feels like a current-generation admin product.
acceptanceCriteria: .admin-drawer: clean rail with var(--border-subtle) edge and comfortable item spacing (pick white or dark navy, consistent)

.admin-nav-item: var(--radius-md) hover bg; active = var(--blue-50) bg + var(--blue) text/icon + left accent bar or pill highlight; smooth transitions; keep all data-admin-section attributes and showAdminSection() wiring
.admin-drawer-toggle: rounded hover state
.admin-page-header and admin .op-welcome: clear page title + subtitle hierarchy

Dashboard stat cards: grep js/app modules for classes rendered into .admin-summary-bar and #admin-platform-stats; style as white rounded cards (var(--radius-lg), var(--shadow-sm), hover var(--shadow-md)) with 24px+ bold navy metrics and small uppercase labels
.admin-main: consistent max-width, padding, and section spacing
Drawer still toggles via toggleAdminDrawer(); layout responsive at 768px
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (dashboard)

userStories.title: Admin console tables, forms and remaining sections redesign
userStories.description: As an admin, I want readable modern tables and forms across Users, Live Map, Add Member, Companies, Pricing, Revenue, Compliance, Drones, Logs, and Settings.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin tables (grep js/app modules for the table/row classes used across admin sections): header row 12px uppercase var(--gray-400) semibold with bottom border; body rows 14px with var(--border-subtle) dividers only (no full grid borders); row hover var(--gray-50); tables wrapped in rounded white cards with overflow-x auto
Status/role indicators in tables become colored rounded chips (soft green/blue/red/gray backgrounds)
Table action buttons: compact rounded buttons with hover states
Add Member and Settings forms: inputs/selects 44-48px rounded with focus ring (match US-002 input language); labels 13px medium var(--gray-600); grouped in rounded card sections
Pricing/Revenue/Compliance/Drones/Logs sections wrapped in the consistent card language; .pd-loading and empty states styled softly
Log output styled as a rounded code block (var(--gray-50) or dark)
Every admin section still renders its data (no behavior changes)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (Users table and Add Member form)

userStories.title: Responsive and interaction polish sweep (all views)
userStories.description: As any user on any device, I want the app to be responsive and interactions to feel smooth so nothing looks broken or janky.
acceptanceCriteria: No horizontal scroll or broken/overlapping layout at 360px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px in all four views (fix findings)
All interactive elements have hover, active, focus-visible, and (where relevant) disabled states - sweep for stragglers
Custom scrollbar styling for panels/lists: thin rounded gray thumb (WebKit ::-webkit-scrollbar + scrollbar-width)
Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block disabling non-essential animations/transitions
Body text and labels meet WCAG AA contrast against their backgrounds (spot-fix violations)
Touch targets >=40px for primary mobile actions in booking/tracking panels
Spacing audit: panel sections, card paddings, and gaps follow a 4px-based rhythm
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (360px and 1440px screenshots of all four views)

userStories.title: Auth and onboarding microcopy upgrade
userStories.description: As a new or returning user, I want warm, confident copy on the auth screens so the product feels polished and human instead of terse and mechanical.
acceptanceCriteria: Login card: heading 'Sign in' becomes 'Welcome back'; subtext 'to continue to IraGo' becomes 'Sign in to book your next flight' (update BOTH the static markup in app.html #login-card-sub AND the portalCopy strings in js/app/02-auth.js around lines 54-80)

Role-specific subtexts improved too: operator 'to continue to the IraGo Pilot Console' becomes 'Sign in to your Pilot Console'; admin 'to continue to the IraGo Admin Console' becomes 'Sign in to the Admin Console' (keep applyPortalLabels/switchLoginRole wiring intact)
Register card: subtext 'Enter your details and verify your email to get started.' becomes benefit-led copy, e.g. 'Book eVTOL air taxis across India in minutes.'

OTP cards: 'Enter the code from your email.' style strings become clearer, e.g. 'We emailed you a 6-digit code. It expires in 10 minutes.' (match actual expiry if defined in src/otp.js, otherwise omit the expiry claim)
Forgot password: 'Enter your email and we'll send a verification code.' becomes reassuring, e.g. 'No worries - enter your email and we'll send you a reset code.'
Generic error strings on auth cards reviewed: no raw server errors shown; common cases read friendly (e.g. 'Incorrect email or password')
Placeholder text sweep on auth inputs: 'you@example.com' kept, but any bare/technical placeholders made human
All changed JS strings edited in js/app/*.js (never the bundle) followed by npm run build:js; no smart/curly quotes introduced
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (login + register + forgot cards, all three role tabs)

userStories.title: Booking, tracking and payment microcopy upgrade
userStories.description: As a passenger, I want clear, encouraging copy through search, booking, tracking, and payment so every step feels guided.
acceptanceCriteria: Location inputs: placeholder 'Enter pickup location' becomes 'Pickup from...' and 'Enter destination' becomes 'Where to?' (app.html #pickup-input / #dest-input)

Search button 'Find Available Rides' reviewed and tightened, e.g. 'Search flights' (check js/app/06-booking.js for code that rewrites #search-btn-text per service tab and keep those variants consistent)
Rides header 'Available Rides' becomes service-aware or friendlier, e.g. 'Choose your flight'; grep JS for any code writing #rides-title before changing
Tracking empty state: 'No active ride' / 'Live tracking will appear here after booking.' becomes warmer, e.g. 'No flights yet' / 'Book a ride and you will see live tracking here.'
Tracking OTP label 'Share this OTP with your pilot to start ride' becomes grammatical and clear, e.g. 'Share this code with your pilot to start your ride'
Payment: 'Tap Pay to confirm your ride and notify nearby pilots.' and demo notes reviewed for clarity; confirmation card 'Your ride is being prepared' becomes e.g. 'We are lining up your aircraft'
Rating prompt 'Optional comment (optional)' redundancy fixed to 'Add a comment (optional)'
Empty/loading strings in booking + drone panels ('Loading drone services...', 'Loading your bookings...') get consistent sentence-case friendly phrasing
Strings that live in JS are changed in js/app/*.js with npm run build:js afterwards; strings in app.html changed in place; no ids/classes/handlers touched
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (booking panel, tracking panel, payment overlay)

userStories.title: Operator and admin microcopy and empty-state upgrade
userStories.description: As a pilot or admin, I want purposeful headings, statuses, and empty states so the consoles read professionally.
acceptanceCriteria: Operator: duty card copy reviewed - status strings (grep js/app/04-operator.js for what fills #op-duty-status/#op-duty-sub) read clearly, e.g. 'You are on duty - receiving dispatch offers' / 'You are off duty - go on duty to receive trips'
Operator empty states ('Loading zones...', 'Loading earnings...', etc.) and any 'No trips' states get consistent friendly phrasing with a hint of what will appear there
Admin dashboard: 'Overview' subtitle becomes informative, e.g. 'Everything happening across IraGo right now'; section titles reviewed for consistency (sentence case, no trailing colons)
Admin empty/loading states ('Loading platform stats...', 'Loading users...', 'Loading live flights...', 'Loading fleet...') unified to one consistent pattern across all 11 sections
All admin table empty results show a helpful empty state (e.g. 'No users match your search') instead of blank space - check the JS renderers for what they output when lists are empty
Page title in app.html <title> reviewed: 'IraGo - Book Your Air Taxi' kept or improved
JS-owned strings edited in js/app/*.js + npm run build:js; no ids/classes/handlers touched; no smart quotes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (operator console + 2-3 admin sections)

userStories.title: Google Maps style route search: destination-first, current location as default source
userStories.description: As a passenger, I want to just type where I am going - like Google Maps - with my current location assumed as the pickup point, so booking takes one step instead of two.
acceptanceCriteria: On entering the booking view logged in (and no active/restored ride), keyboard focus lands in the destination input (#dest-input), not the pickup input - it is the primary action

Pickup auto-fill from GPS already exists in js/app/02-auth.js (silent getCurrentPosition after map load) - verify it works and, when it fills, the pickup input visually reads as 'Your location' state: blue dot indicator and the reverse-geocoded name (keep existing setPickup naming)
If geolocation is denied/unavailable, pickup input placeholder becomes 'Choose pickup location' and no error toast/alert is shown on load; the inline GPS button remains as the retry affordance

Add a 'Use current location' row pinned at the top of the pickup suggestions dropdown (#pickup-suggest) whenever it opens, with a GPS icon, wired to the existing useCurrentLocation() - grep js/app/05-map.js for how suggestion rows are rendered and follow that pattern
Searching with a destination set but no pickup shows a friendly inline prompt ('Set your pickup point') and focuses/highlights the pickup input instead of failing silently
When destination is chosen first and pickup is auto-filled, the map fits the route bounds automatically (existing drawRoute behavior verified, not rewritten)
Visual order stays pickup-above-destination (route metaphor); only focus priority and defaults change - no element ids, handlers, or swapLocations()/startMapPick() wiring changed
Autofocus must not steal focus when a tracking session is restored or when an overlay (payment/confirm/profile) is open
Changes in js/app/*.js followed by npm run build:js; no smart quotes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (fresh load with geolocation granted and denied)

userStories.title: Marketplace-style ride results: operator company as the trust signal (Google Flights / Uber inspiration)
userStories.description: As a passenger choosing between flights, I want to see which operator company runs each ride - the way Google Flights leads with the airline - so I can choose on trust, not just price.
acceptanceCriteria: Dispatch/search already returns company data (grep js/app/06-booking.js for where ride cards are rendered and what fields the API returns; src/dispatch.js exposes companyName/companyCode/companyRating) - surface it: each ride card shows an operator company chip (company name + small code monogram) and company rating stars when available

Ride card layout follows flight-search conventions: company chip top-left, aircraft type + pilot name as secondary line, ETA/duration as metadata, price right-aligned and most prominent (18px+ bold navy)
Rides with no company affiliation show a neutral 'Independent operator' chip so the layout never collapses
Tracking pilot card upgraded: company name becomes a visible chip/badge next to the pilot name (not a gray afterthought line) - #tracking-pilot-company content unchanged, presentation upgraded
Booking confirmation card includes the operating company line (grep what data currentBooking._company holds and render it)

If the ride-search API response does NOT include company fields, extend the server response minimally (src/dispatch.js already selects them for dispatch - reuse the same JOIN for the search/quote endpoint) without changing existing response fields
JS edits in js/app/*.js + npm run build:js; no ids/handlers renamed; no smart quotes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (ride results showing company chips)

userStories.title: Terminology cleanup: Pilot = person, Operator = company (aggregator model)
userStories.description: As IraGo, we partner with operator companies who employ pilots (unlike Uber's direct-driver model) - the UI language must reflect that consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Login role switcher: the 'Operator' tab label becomes 'Pilot' (data-role attribute value and all JS role strings stay 'operator' - this is a LABEL-ONLY change; grep js/app/02-auth.js portalCopy/applyPortalLabels and update display strings)
Login subtext for pilots reads accordingly (e.g. 'Sign in to your Pilot Console')
Admin Users section: tab 'Operators' becomes 'Pilots' (#admin-tab-operator label only); 'Operators Live Map' section title becomes 'Pilots Live Map'
Admin Add Member: role dropdown option label 'Operator' becomes 'Pilot' (option value stays 'operator'); form heading/copy refers to adding a pilot or admin
Operator profile card role chip shows 'Pilot' instead of 'Operator' (find where profile-display-role is filled in js/app and map the label at display time only)
Admin Companies section keeps 'Operator Companies' naming - companies are the operators; add a one-line explainer under the title: 'Operator companies employ the pilots who fly IraGo trips.'
Server role values, API params, DB columns, and all code identifiers are NOT renamed - display labels only; grep for user-visible strings, not variables
JS edits in js/app/*.js + npm run build:js; typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (login tabs, admin users tabs, add member form)

userStories.title: Pilot-to-company assignment in admin (Add Member + Users list)
userStories.description: As an admin, I must assign every pilot to an operator company and regional office when creating them, and see/filter affiliation in the Users list - pilots cannot float unaffiliated in an aggregator.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Member form: when role = Pilot(operator), show two selects - Operator company (required, loaded from the existing companies endpoint used by the Companies section) and Regional office (optional, filtered by chosen company via the existing GET offices-by-company endpoint in src/admin-routes.js ~line 527)
When role = Admin, the company/office selects are hidden

doAddUser in js/app/03-admin-profile.js sends companyId/officeId; extend the admin add-user endpoint in src/admin-routes.js to accept and persist optional companyId/officeId on the created user (columns already exist on users table - see src/demo-routes.js INSERT for reference)
Server validates companyId exists when provided; returns friendly error otherwise
Admin Users list: pilot rows show a company column/chip (JOIN operator_companies in the users listing query if not already present); pilots without a company show 'Unassigned' in amber
Users list gets a company filter dropdown (client-side filtering over the loaded list is acceptable)
Existing pilots with NULL company continue to work everywhere (no NOT NULL migration)
JS edits in js/app/*.js + npm run build:js; server starts clean (node server.js boots)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (Add Member with company select, Users list with company chips)

userStories.title: Companies section as a partner directory (Stripe Dashboard inspiration)
userStories.description: As an admin, I want the Operator Companies section to read like a partner-account directory - counts, offices, and health at a glance - not a bare name list.
acceptanceCriteria: Company cards redesigned as rich partner cards: company name + code monogram avatar, HQ city, and live counts - pilots (COUNT users by companyId), regional offices (officeCount already computed in src/admin-routes.js ~line 484), and company rating if present
Extend the companies listing endpoint with a pilotCount subquery if it lacks one (mirror the existing officeCount pattern)
Clicking/expanding a company reveals its regional offices inline (reuse the existing offices-by-company endpoint and current offices list rendering)
Add Company form upgraded to the new form language and includes optional company code field if the DB column exists (check operator_companies schema in scripts/db-init.js first; skip if absent)
Empty state for zero companies: friendly prompt 'Add your first operator company to start onboarding pilots'
Offices list rows show city, address, contact - styled as table/list rows per the admin table language
JS edits in js/app/*.js + npm run build:js; server boots clean; typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (Companies section with counts and expanded offices)

userStories.title: Admin dashboard visual overhaul — verified without a browser (static audit)
userStories.description: As an admin, I want the admin console to actually look modern (not stone-age). US-008/US-009 restyled the CSS but were marked passing without any verification. This environment cannot drive a browser, so verification here is a STATIC audit of markup + CSS, producing a human-reviewable evidence report — no screenshots, no headless browser.
acceptanceCriteria: DESIGN REFERENCE (target aesthetic) — image saved at tasks/refs/us019-dashboard-reference.png, source: https://cdn.dribbble.com/userupload/42549517/file/original-0ac92b771b8ded6724134a04f2e9a49d.png?resize=752x&vertical=center. Match these concrete traits (the loop is headless, so treat this as the spec): (1) soft light-gray page background (~#F3F4F6) with all content on WHITE rounded cards, large radius ~16-20px (--radius-lg) and very soft shadows (--shadow-sm), generous whitespace/padding ~20-24px; (2) left nav: white rail, icon+label items, ACTIVE item is a soft rounded-pill highlight (light gray/blue-50) not a hard bar, muted gray inactive icons; (3) top header: bold page title left, pill-shaped search input, a solid BLACK rounded primary button ('Create'-style), round icon buttons + avatar on the right; (4) stat cards: small icon+label on top, VERY large bold near-black metric number, a soft-background colored delta chip (green up / red down) and muted 'vs last month' subtext; (5) status indicators rendered as soft rounded chips (green=Active, gray=Offline/muted) — never plain text; (6) lists (products/comments) as clean rows with thumbnail/avatar, title, muted meta, right-aligned value, divider-only separators (no full grid borders); outlined pill 'View all' buttons; (7) accent color green for positive + neutral grays everywhere else; rounded EVERYTHING (cards, inputs, chips, buttons); thin subtle borders (--border-subtle), no heavy 1px grid lines

Enumerate every admin section (Dashboard, Users, Add Member, Pricing, Revenue, Compliance, Drones, Logs, Settings, Companies) and identify the container/root class each one renders with in app.html and js/app/03-admin-profile.js / js/app/09-drones.js

Root-cause audit via grep (no browser): for each admin section find (a) leftover inline style= attributes on admin elements, (b) legacy pre-redesign class names not covered by US-008/US-009, (c) hardcoded colors/px instead of design tokens (var(--...)). List every offender with file:line

Fix the offenders: migrate inline styles and legacy classes onto the modern token-based classes (.admin-drawer, .admin-nav-item, .pd-stat, .admin-page-header, .admin-table, .admin-table-wrap, .pd-input, .op-status-badge, .admin-form-card) so every section uses --surface, --border-subtle, --shadow-sm/md, --radius-lg, --blue-50 accents, uppercase micro-labels and bold navy metrics

Static assertion 1 — coverage: for each admin section's root class, grep css/app.css and confirm a matching rule exists that sets token-based background/border/radius (quote the actual CSS rule as evidence)
Static assertion 2 — no regressions: grep confirms ZERO remaining inline style= on admin dashboard/table/card elements and ZERO hardcoded hex colors in admin CSS blocks (all via var(--...))

Write tasks/us019-admin-audit.md: a table with one row per admin section — [section | root class | modern CSS rule found (yes/quote) | inline/legacy offenders before | offenders after (must be 0)]. This file IS the verification evidence in place of screenshots
Bump the ?v= cache-buster on css/app.css and app.bundle.js in app.html; rebuild the bundle with npm run build:js
Typecheck passes (npm run typecheck)

MANDATORY gate: only set passes:true if tasks/us019-admin-audit.md exists AND every section row shows a modern token-based CSS rule AND the 'offenders after' column is 0 for every section. Record the audit-file path in notes. Do NOT claim browser/screenshot verification — this story is verified by the static audit only
userStories.notes: Static audit at tasks/us019-admin-audit.md. All 11 admin sections verified: 0 inline style= in HTML, 0 hardcoded hex in admin CSS, all using token-based classes (--surface, --border-subtle, --shadow-sm, --radius-lg, etc.). Added amber/purple tokens, 30+ utility CSS classes, removed duplicate badge rules.

Grouped nav: OVERVIEW (Dashboard, Live Map), MANAGEMENT (Users, Companies, Drones), FINANCE (Pricing, Revenue), GOVERNANCE (Compliance), SYSTEM (Settings, Logs) pinned to bottom. Add Member demoted to button in Users section header.
userStories.title: Admin console information architecture: grouped nav + demoted System zone
userStories.description: As an admin, the left nav is 11 flat equally-weighted items (Dashboard, Users, Live Map, Add Member, Logs, Companies, Pricing, Revenue, Compliance, Drones, Settings) with no grouping or hierarchy, mixing a one-off action (Add Member) and dev/technical items (Logs, Settings) with core destinations — it's cluttered and confusing.
acceptanceCriteria: Reorganize the 11 .admin-nav-item buttons in #admin-drawer into labeled groups with small uppercase group headers (new non-interactive .admin-nav-group-label, ~11px, var(--gray-400)), preserving EVERY data-admin-section value and showAdminSection() wiring: OVERVIEW = Dashboard, Live Map; MANAGEMENT = Users, Companies, Drones; FINANCE = Pricing, Revenue; GOVERNANCE = Compliance; SYSTEM = Settings, Logs

Pin the SYSTEM group to the BOTTOM of the drawer (margin-top:auto) and style it visually de-emphasized/muted with a divider above, so Logs/Settings read as advanced/technical, not primary destinations

Demote 'Add Member' from a top-level nav item to a primary action button inside the Users section header (e.g. a '+ Add member' button that opens the existing showAdminSection('add') target); remove it from the drawer nav list
All 11 sections still open via their existing data-admin-section/showAdminSection wiring; active-state highlighting still works; toggleAdminDrawer() still works; responsive at 768px
Rebuild bundle (npm run build:js), bump ?v= cache-buster; typecheck passes
Record the final grouping in progress.txt notes

Browser-free verification: this environment cannot render a browser, so verify structurally — grep the markup/JS for the required structure and write the before/after flow + evidence into progress.txt notes. Do NOT claim visual/screenshot verification. Confirm each data-admin-section still present and grouped under the correct header, Add Member is no longer an .admin-nav-item, and the SYSTEM group is the last group in the drawer.

userStories.notes: Progressive disclosure: popular routes behind collapsible toggle, service banners/extras placed below CTA as subordinate, ride details (carbon comparison + route info) in collapsible Flight Details section. Ride cards restructured: price+time at top, company chip inline, stats as subdued secondary metadata. Extra-field inputs upgraded to 44px/radius-md/focus-ring.
userStories.title: Route & flight search UX overhaul: destination-first, progressive disclosure, intuitive results
userStories.description: As a passenger, searching routes/flights is not intuitive — inputs and results present too much at once with no clear primary path. Building on US-014 (destination-first route search) and US-015 (marketplace results), make the whole search-to-result flow feel obvious and calm.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit the current route/flight search + results components in the booking view and write the current step-by-step flow and its friction points (competing CTAs, fields shown before relevant, unclear next action) into progress.txt

Establish ONE primary path per step: (1) where to (destination) with current location as default source, (2) when/service refinement secondary, (3) a single dominant primary CTA to search — secondary controls (filters, service variants, extras) must be visually subordinate and progressively disclosed, not shown all at once

Search results (ride/flight cards) must lead with the decision-driving info (price, time, operator/trust signal) in a scannable hierarchy; de-emphasize secondary metadata; every empty/loading/no-results state states the next action (ties to US-012 microcopy)
Inputs use the modern token-based form language (44-48px rounded, focus ring, clear labels); no raw/unstyled search fields remain
Preserve all switchService()/search/booking JS wiring; no behavior regressions; rebuild bundle + cache-bust; typecheck passes

Browser-free verification: this environment cannot render a browser, so verify structurally — grep the markup/JS for the required structure and write the before/after flow + evidence into progress.txt notes. Do NOT claim visual/screenshot verification. Confirm the single-primary-CTA structure, progressive-disclosure containers, and results-card hierarchy exist in the markup, and document the redesigned flow in progress.txt.

userStories.notes: Step-by-step flow: search panel hides when results show, back button added to rides-header, clear headings at each step (Where are you going? / Choose your flight / Live Tracking / Review & Offers / Payment / Booking Confirmed!), one primary CTA per step.
userStories.title: Passenger booking end-to-end flow & wayfinding
userStories.description: As a passenger, the booking journey across search -> results -> details -> payment -> tracking should feel like one guided flow with clear progress, not disconnected screens.
acceptanceCriteria: Map the full passenger journey (service select -> search -> results -> ride detail -> payment -> confirmation -> live tracking) and document each transition and any dead-ends or unclear steps in progress.txt
Ensure every step has a clear title/heading so the user always knows where they are, one obvious primary action, and an obvious way back; no step dead-ends
Reduce simultaneous choices: apply progressive disclosure so only the current decision is prominent; move advanced/rarely-used options behind a secondary affordance
Consistent primary/secondary button hierarchy across all booking steps (one primary CTA per screen)
Preserve all booking JS wiring; no regressions; rebuild + cache-bust; typecheck passes

Browser-free verification: this environment cannot render a browser, so verify structurally — grep the markup/JS for the required structure and write the before/after flow + evidence into progress.txt notes. Do NOT claim visual/screenshot verification. Provide grep evidence that each step has a heading + single primary CTA, and document the journey in progress.txt.

userStories.notes: Added section nav with 5 items in 3 groups: FLIGHTS (Trips - primary default), OPERATIONS (Pre-flight, Altitude), OVERVIEW/system-zone (Earnings, Account). Duty card stays always visible above nav. Section switching via showOperatorSection(). Wayfinding headings on each section.
userStories.title: Operator / Pilot console information architecture & task-focused flow
userStories.description: As a pilot/operator, the console should lead with my primary job (see & manage assigned flights) and tuck secondary/technical sections away, mirroring the admin IA cleanup.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit #operator-view (the 'Pilot Console') sections and nav and document the current sections + flow + friction in progress.txt
Make the pilot's primary task the default landing section and visually dominant; group secondary sections and de-emphasize technical/config ones
If the operator nav has 5+ flat items, apply the same grouped-nav + bottom SYSTEM-zone pattern and .admin-nav-group-label styling established in US-020
Operator view shows a clear current-section heading (wayfinding) like the admin .op-welcome
Preserve all operator JS wiring; no regressions; rebuild + cache-bust; typecheck passes

Browser-free verification: this environment cannot render a browser, so verify structurally — grep the markup/JS for the required structure and write the before/after flow + evidence into progress.txt notes. Do NOT claim visual/screenshot verification. Grep evidence of grouped/prioritized operator nav + heading; document in progress.txt.

userStories.notes: IA guidelines at tasks/ia-guidelines.md. Operator de-emphasized zone renamed from 'Overview' to 'Personal' to avoid collision with admin's primary 'Overview' group. All roles conform: grouped nav (where applicable), wayfinding headings on every section, de-emphasized system/personal zone, no orphaned sections.
userStories.title: Cross-role navigation hierarchy & wayfinding consistency (+ IA guidelines)
userStories.description: As a user in any role, navigation should follow one consistent model: primary destinations prominent, advanced/technical/config consistently placed in a de-emphasized System area, and I always know where I am.
acceptanceCriteria: Define and document ONE navigation model in a new tasks/ia-guidelines.md: primary vs secondary vs system grouping, group-label styling, wayfinding (current-section heading), and single-primary-CTA rule — this doc is the reference for all future pages

Verify each role (passenger, operator, admin) conforms: technical/config/logs live in a consistent de-emphasized location; every view has a current-location heading; group-label styling is shared/consistent
Fix any role that doesn't conform (reuse the shared .admin-nav-group-label / heading patterns)
No orphaned sections (every section reachable from its role nav); typecheck passes; rebuild + cache-bust if markup changed

Browser-free verification: this environment cannot render a browser, so verify structurally — grep the markup/JS for the required structure and write the before/after flow + evidence into progress.txt notes.
